# VER-QUIC-CRT-0010 - QUIC Server-Role Managed TLS Crypto Floor Verification

## Scope

This slice verifies only the permanent server-role crypto floor from peer ClientHello through local ServerHello and Handshake packet-protection material availability. It does not prove EncryptedExtensions, certificate flight, server Finished, client-certificate authentication, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0107
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the narrow server-role ClientHello to ServerHello crypto floor behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully.
- The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the supported server-role ClientHello path, outbound ServerHello exposure, Handshake key-availability publication ordering, malformed framing rejection, unsupported version or cipher-suite rejection, unsupported named-group or key-share rejection, and repeated or conflicting progression rejection.
- Run the RFC9001 requirement-home tests that still cover outbound Handshake packet emission, Handshake packet protection, transport-parameter staging, and deterministic rejection of malformed Handshake packet input on the bridge seam.
- Inspect the bridge driver, transcript-progress owner, and key-schedule implementation to confirm the slice stays inside the main library seam, keeps runtime or host wiring unchanged, keeps commit unavailable, and does not add native TLS fallback.

## Expected Result

The server role accepts only the supported ClientHello subset for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, preserves staged peer transport parameters from that ClientHello, constructs one local ServerHello, exposes those bytes through the existing Handshake crypto-output seam, publishes explicit Handshake open/protect material and Handshake key availability only after the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary exists, rejects malformed or unsupported ClientHello progression deterministically, and still leaves server-role commit and wider handshake support unavailable.
